description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages (build, test, analysis) and global variables BUILD_IMAGES_PROJECT, FEDORA_BUILD, MINGW_BUILD, TUMBLEWEED_BUILD, UBUNTU_BUILD. In the test stage define visualstudio/x86 and visualstudio/x86_64 (both extend .vs, run on Windows runners, execute PowerShell before_script to create .vcpkg\\archives and obj, then cmake -A win32 or win64 -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug -DCMAKE_TOOLCHAIN_FILE=C:/vcpkg/scripts/buildsystems/vcpkg.cmake -DPICKY_DEVELOPER=ON -DUNIT_TESTING=ON .., cmake --build ., ctest -C Debug --output-on-failure), fedora/clang/x86_64, fedora/gcc/x86_64, fedora/memory-sanitizer, fedora/undefined-sanitizer (all use image $CI_REGISTRY/$BUILD_IMAGES_PROJECT:$FEDORA_BUILD and run mkdir -p obj && cd obj && cmake with appropriate -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE and compiler flags, then make -j$(nproc) && ctest --output-on-failure), freebsd/x86_64 (image null, only branches of cmocka/cmocka and cryptomilk/cmocka, runs similar cmake, make and ctest), ubuntu/x86_64 (image $CI_REGISTRY/$BUILD_IMAGES_PROJECT:$UBUNTU_BUILD, same script), tumbleweed/x86/gcc, tumbleweed/x86/gcc7, tumbleweed/x86_64/clang, tumbleweed/x86_64/gcc, tumbleweed/x86_64/gcc7 (all use image $CI_REGISTRY/$BUILD_IMAGES_PROJECT:$TUMBLEWEED_BUILD with appropriate toolchain or compiler settings, then make and ctest), and each job caches no additional paths, uploads obj/ artifacts that expire in one week and are kept on failure, and excludes tags. In the build stage add fedora/address-sanitizer (image $...:$FEDORA_BUILD, script mkdir -p obj && cd obj && cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=AddressSanitizer -DPICKY_DEVELOPER=ON -DUNIT_TESTING=ON .. && make -j$(nproc) && ctest --output-on-failure), fedora/build (image $...:$FEDORA_BUILD, script pushd coverity && make && popd && mkdir -p obj && cd obj && cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/tmp/cmocka -DPICKY_DEVELOPER=ON .. && make -j$(nproc) && make install), fedora/meson (image $...:$FEDORA_BUILD, script meson build -Dunit_testing=true && meson test -C build --print-errorlogs), fedora/mingw32 and fedora/mingw64 (image $...:$MINGW_BUILD, script set WINEPATH, WINEDEBUG, XDG_RUNTIME_DIR then run mingw32-cmake or mingw64-cmake with RelWithDebInfo, make and ctest), and tumbleweed/docs (image $...:$TUMBLEWEED_BUILD, script mkdir -p obj && cd obj && cmake .. && make docs), each publishing obj/ artifacts on failure. In the analysis stage add coverity (image $...:$FEDORA_BUILD, only on master or schedules when COVERITY_SCAN_TOKEN, COVERITY_SCAN_PROJECT_NAME and COVERITY_SCAN_EMAIL are set, script downloads the Coverity tool, runs cmake, cov-build, packages cov-int and uploads via curl), fedora/coverage (image $...:$FEDORA_BUILD, script builds with CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Profiling, runs make ExperimentalTest && make ExperimentalCoverage, defines coverage regex), fedora/csbuild (image $...:$FEDORA_BUILD, script computes CI_COMMIT_RANGE and runs csbuild with custom options), tumbleweed/static-analysis (image $...:$TUMBLEWEED_BUILD, script sets CCC_CC/CCC_CXX to clang, runs scan-build cmake and scan-build make), each defining one‑week expiring artifacts (obj/, obj/cov-int/*.txt, obj/scan) kept on failure and excluding tags, with no deployment targets.
